chrome_shortlist.name: POLARIS Layer-2 deterministic markdown/PDF post-filter (EXTEND clean_fetch_body) — PRIMARY, the only fix that lands TODAY
chrome_shortlist.why: VERIFIED IN-TREE: POLARIS's three live fetch backends (Crawl4AI/Jina/Firecrawl) ALL emit MARKDOWN (access_bypass.py:13-15), and clean_fetch_body already runs on that markdown at live_retriever.py:2136 BEFORE _build_provenance_quote builds the span. Two of the residual samples ('Markdown Content: ## 1. Introduction', 'Published Time: 2024-09-04') are literal Jina-reader markdown-preamble signatures, proving chrome enters through the markdown path. Every Layer-1 DOM extractor is USELESS on this input — they need raw HTML POLARIS does not capture. This deterministic allowlist extension (anchored whole-line / line-prefix rules for classes 1-4 + a dictionary-validated glued-token rejoin for class 5b 'Governan; ce') is the ONLY candidate that improves the real current pipeline without a fetch-path rebuild. Deterministic+fast = sovereignty-perfect, run-speed-perfect. Faithfulness-safe: allowlist-only, and the render-seam is FLAG-not-delete. Do NOT rank chrome by WCXB F1 (banned metric-as-quality per §-1.1): WCXB measures general main-content extraction on RAW HTML across 7 page types, not our 6 classes on our markdown.
chrome_shortlist.integration_point: src/tools/access_bypass.py clean_fetch_body (CleanedFetch at line 1880; _LINE_WRAP_HYPHEN_RE at 1944 already handles class-5a line-wrap hyphen). Called from live_retriever.py:2136 before the span is built. Class-5b glued-token rejoin = NEW deterministic dictionary check (dehyphen will NOT catch a semicolon-insertion artifact).

chrome_shortlist.name: Deterministic FLAG-not-drop render-seam screen (is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable) — BACKSTOP, port iwire013 forensic rules
chrome_shortlist.why: VERIFIED present at weighted_enrichment.py:936. This is NOT a third-party tool; it is the one shared predicate that WITHHOLDS chrome/truncated render units from the rendered rollup but NEVER deletes them from evidence (§-1.3 clinical-safety FLAG-not-drop). Per the I-wire-013 memory pin, the production predicate is BLIND to mid-prose glued ToC/author/license chrome and cut-word-before-[N] truncation; the independent forensic detector (scripts/iwire013_sec11_forensic_audit.py) reproduced 55 chrome / 20 truncation where the production predicate reported ~0. Port the detector's CONTAINMENT rules (glued inline headers, affiliation-middot lists, dotted-ToC tokens, bare-DOI/ORCID rows, masthead/license regexes) INTO the predicate. The advisor's point stands: a render screen 'can't hit zero', so this is the safety net that catches chrome welded mid-paragraph that the Layer-2 line-filter and any extractor miss — it complements, never replaces, the upstream fix.
chrome_shortlist.integration_point: src/polaris_graph/generator/weighted_enrichment.py is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable + the single render-seam chokepoint over every claim unit; feeds the chrome-as-claim fail-loud canary. FLAG-not-drop only.

chrome_shortlist.name: SymSpell (symspellpy 6.9.0) + wordfreq validator + keep-hyphen regex — POINT FIX for class-5b intra-word glue ONLY
chrome_shortlist.why: The honest unique fit for the one class NO generic extractor and NO standard de-hyphenation touches: intra-word semicolon/space glue ('Governan; ce', 'Agricultural Oc; b'). Both chrome agents independently converged here. GROBID/MinerU de-hyphenation handle only trailing line-end hyphens (-\n); dehyphen (PyPI, GPLv3, Flair char-LM, German-biased, pre-2024) also only handles line-end hyphens and is REJECTED. The recommended pipeline is deterministic: keep-hyphen regex FIRST (close 'high- risk' → 'high-risk', preserve the hyphen so legit compounds aren't over-merged) THEN SymSpell LookupCompound/WordSegmentation for the spurious-glue case, with wordfreq as the merge-validator (only close if the joined token is a real word). Used naively SymSpell over-merges legit hyphenated compounds — so it MUST be gated behind the keep-hyphen regex + dictionary check. symspellpy is MIT (2025-03-09); wordfreq 3.1.1 is 2023 = kept ONLY as a stable offline validator utility (incumbent baseline), not a frontier claim. Offline, CPU, sovereignty-clean.
chrome_shortlist.integration_point: A shared text-normalize helper called from access_bypass.clean_fetch_body (Layer-2) + live_retriever._build_provenance_quote, before spans are stored.
chrome_shortlist.sovereignty: open_selfhostable (deterministic, no GPU; wordfreq=2023 stable validator)

dedup_shortlist.name: Content-word shingle Jaccard 0.82 + polarity guard (IN-TREE _build_prose_groups, enable PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE) — deterministic cheap PRE-FILTER
dedup_shortlist.why: VERIFIED IN-TREE and DEFAULT-OFF (fact_dedup.py:450). VERIFIED REACHABLE for the showcased FIX-D example: I read extract_signature/FactSignature.is_empty (fact_dedup.py:265-303) — is_empty is True unless there is a %-suffixed decimal, a $-amount, or a 19xx/20xx year. Bare integer '702' matches NONE, and group-2 has no number, so BOTH FIX-D groups produce an EMPTY signature and route to the PROSE path (line 471-472 skips non-empty/numeric sentences). The prose path clusters on >=2 occurrences INTRA-OR-cross section (line 500), so it catches the intra-section FIX-D repeats. The polarity guard (line 489) blocks antonym false-merges ('raised' vs 'lowered'/'not') even at Jaccard ~1.0. SAFE failure mode: its only error is UNDER-merge (the two FIX-D groups share only the 'probability computerisation' bigram, far below 0.82 → can never cross-merge → 2 survivors guaranteed). Within group-1 the 6 verb-varied paraphrases may dip below 0.82 and partially survive — which is exactly why the NLI confirmer leg exists. Fully deterministic, CPU, sub-ms at our scale.
dedup_shortlist.integration_point: fact_dedup.build_groups → _build_prose_groups (gate PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE, threshold PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE_JACCARD). Reached from multi_section_generator.py:7675 dedup_pass on the composed section_results.

dedup_shortlist.name: Bidirectional / mutual-entailment NLI (IN-TREE consolidation_nli.py via _build_nli_prose_groups, enable PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI + PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_PROSE) — SEMANTIC confirmer
dedup_shortlist.why: VERIFIED IN-TREE and DEFAULT-OFF (fact_dedup.py:546, gated 513-525). It is the ONLY candidate that PASSES the discriminating 2-groups-not-1 test AND whose failure mode is the SAFE direction. Two reps merge only when entailment is the argmax in BOTH directions above a margin = semantic EQUIVALENCE (not a cosine threshold, not one-way entailment). The FIX-D method-claim does NOT mutually entail the definition-claim → the two groups stay SEPARATE (2 survivors); within each group the 6/5 paraphrases mutually entail → each collapses to one. Antonym flips score CONTRADICTION (argmax) = free polarity safety. Catches the divergent-verb paraphrases ('introduced/developed/employed') that Jaccard misses. Open-weight deBERTa runs on the VM's local GPU (sovereignty OK per the local-GPU rule). HONESTY: no clean external 2025-2026 primary validates 'bidirectional-NLI-as-equivalence-dedup' specifically (arXiv:2508.17127 is one-directional/targeted and by its own abstract does NOT address equivalence detection); the candidate rests on the in-tree bake-off (R=1.0/P=1.0 vs literal-floor R=0.0) plus verified 2025 sources establishing semantic-over-lexical necessity. Pairwise O(n^2) bounded by PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MAX_PAIRS=20000 / MAX_SENTENCES=200.
dedup_shortlist.integration_point: synthesis/consolidation_nli.py group_clusters, wired via fact_dedup._build_nli_prose_groups in build_groups (line 872). Same dedup_pass call path. Layered ON the Jaccard pre-filter AND the same-citation gate + carve-out.
dedup_shortlist.sovereignty: open_selfhostable (open-weight deBERTa, VM GPU only — not the laptop per §8.4)

dedup_shortlist.name: Writer-side prompt constraint 'state each distinct claim once' on abstractive_writer.py — ROOT-CAUSE prevention
dedup_shortlist.why: The only genuinely NEW build (the other two are enable-tune-confirm). Fixes the defect at the SOURCE: the abstractive writer pads ('introduced/developed/employed a methodology' x6) because scope/format are under-specified (documented padding-by-paraphrase behaviour). An explicit instruction + few-shot ('enumerate each DISTINCT verified claim exactly once; one sentence per (subject,predicate,object) basket; do not restate with synonyms') prevents FIX-D from being GENERATED — the writer emits the methodology claim once and the exposure-measure claim once = 2 sentences. Faithfulness-safe BY CONSTRUCTION: it is an instruction to the generator, NOT a post-hoc deletion, so it can never drop a distinct claim. Uses the EXISTING generator-role LLM call governed by polaris_runtime_lock.yaml — no new model, reduces tokens/cost, and drops the downstream dedup load. CAVEAT: prompt adherence is probabilistic, so it MUST be backed by the deterministic+NLI safety net above — never the sole fix. Token-level decoding penalties (DRY/no_repeat_ngram/frequency_penalty) are DISQUALIFIED (verified: they operate on token identity, cannot catch semantic paraphrase).
dedup_shortlist.integration_point: src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py system prompt (the existing generator invocation). Complements, does not replace, verified_compose/fact_dedup post-hoc dedup.
dedup_shortlist.sovereignty: open_selfhostable (uses the existing locked generator; no new dependency)

synthesis.benchmark_plan:
DISCRIMINATOR (NOT WCXB F1 / NOT raw similarity — both banned as metric-as-quality per §-1.1). Chrome rank = handles_our_shapes on OUR markdown input + zero real-content loss. Dedup rank = paraphrase-collapse with the OVER-MERGE/UNDER-MERGE ASYMMETRY: over-merge (collapsing two distinct claims) = distinct-claim loss = faithfulness-LETHAL = auto-DISQUALIFY (zero tolerance); under-merge (leftover repeats) = SAFE/graded. This asymmetry demotes ALL cosine/embedding methods (SemHash, SemDeDup, Qwen3-cosine, MinHash/SimHash/ROUGE) to comparison rows only.

REAL DATA (located + verified on disk): banked replay3 set at C:/POLARIS/outputs/iwire013_validate_local/ {report.md, bibliography.json, corpus_snapshot.json, evidence_pool.json}; the FIX-D 'probability of computerisation' pattern is in that report.md (workforce/displacement = drb_72_ai_labor). Use these as the frozen replay corpus.

CHROME BENCHMARK — TWO TRACKS (the replay3 spans are MARKDOWN; there is NO stored raw HTML, so Layer-1 and Layer-2 CANNOT score on one shared gold):
 Track A (Layer-2, the real apples-to-apples test): score the clean_fetch_body post-filter + SymSpell/regex + the render-seam screen DIRECTLY on the banked replay3 markdown spans (bibliography.json direct_quotes + report.md render units) vs the per-span gold. This is THE benchmark for what ships today.
 Track B (Layer-1 DOM extractors — trafilatura/rs-trafilatura/GROBID/MinerU-HTML — CONDITIONAL): they need raw HTML POLARIS does not bank. Re-fetch the bibliography.json URLs to a held-out raw-HTML fixture set, build a SEPARATE fresh gold on the re-fetched content, and score there — with an explicit content-DRIFT caveat (re-fetched pages differ from the banked run). Do NOT pretend Layer-1 scores on the same spans as Layer-2. This track answers 'is a raw-HTML capture rebuild worth it', not 'what ships now'.
 Chrome metrics per candidate: (1) chrome-removal recall (graded), (2) real-content-span loss (HARD ZERO gate — one real-content span removed/withheld = DISQUALIFY), (3) per-class breakdown over the 6 classes, (4) latency. Layer-2 semantics = REMOVE chrome from body-before-span (gate: zero real content removed). Render-seam semantics = FLAG-not-drop (gate: zero real-content unit withheld AND chrome stays in evidence).

DEDUP BENCHMARK — single track, on the composed report sections (the dedup runs post-composition on tens-to-low-hundreds of sentences within a section). Replay the banked section_results through multi_section_generator's dedup_pass with each candidate flag combination: (i) baseline (all off), (ii) PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE on, (iii) +PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI+PROSE on, (iv) writer-prompt A/B (regen the FIX-D section with vs without the constraint). Metrics: (1) paraphrase-repeats collapsed = reduction in same-claim restatements (graded; target: the 6 method + 5 definition FIX-D paraphrases → 2 survivors), (2) distinct-claim/number-entity loss (HARD ZERO gate — DISQUALIFY on any), (3) 'probability of computerisation' occurrence count (13x → target 2). FIRE-TEST (cheapest de-risk, §-1.4): run consolidation_nli.group_clusters on the EXACT 11 FIX-D sentences and ASSERT exactly 2 survivors (not 1, not 11); ALSO assert the ROUTING — both groups land on the prose path (verified: '702' yields empty FactSignature so it does NOT route to the numeric path). Comparison rows (run but cannot win): SemHash / Qwen3-cosine / SemDeDup / MinHash / SimHash / ROUGE — record where each OVER-merges the two groups to demonstrate the asymmetry empirically.

RESOURCE DISCIPLINE (§8.4 + memory): the NLI deBERTa leg and any embedding comparison-row run on the VM (2xRTX3090Ti), NEVER the laptop. Deterministic legs (Jaccard, Layer-2 filter, render screen) run local. One process at a time; clean up child processes between candidates.
synthesis.gold_labeling_spec:
Build TWO golds (chrome) + ONE gold (dedup) from the banked replay3 set with §-1.1 line-by-line rigor (claim-by-claim, span-by-span, no sampling, no string-presence shortcuts). Both Claude and Codex label independently in parallel; cross-review reconciles (§-1.1 #5).

CHROME GOLD A (Layer-2, on banked markdown — THE shipping gold): enumerate EVERY span in iwire013_validate_local/bibliography.json (direct_quote fields) + every render unit in report.md. Label each at the LINE/segment level: {span_id, source_url, segment_text, label ∈ {real_content, chrome_class_1_journal_html, chrome_class_2_author_affiliation, chrome_class_3_paywall_preview, chrome_class_4_cookie_consent, chrome_class_5a_linewrap_hyphen, chrome_class_5b_glued_token, chrome_class_6_truncation}, char_offset_range}. For mixed segments (chrome welded mid-prose body), label at the sub-span/char-range level so a candidate is scored on the exact chrome substring, not whole-span. real_content = any number/entity/claim-bearing text = the protected set. Cross-reference corpus_snapshot.json to disambiguate class-6 truncation (fragment present in snapshot but cut in the rendered span).

CHROME GOLD B (Layer-1, on re-fetched raw HTML — conditional): for the SAME bibliography.json URLs, re-fetch raw HTML to fixtures, then label main-content-vs-chrome at the DOM-block level with the same 6-class taxonomy. Tag every fixture with a content-drift note (fetch date, hash) since re-fetched ≠ banked.

DEDUP GOLD (on composed sections): take the FIX-D section (Empirical_Displacement, 'probability of computerisation') + 2-3 other replay3 sections. For each composed sentence record {sentence_id, section, text, claim_id (paraphrases of the SAME claim share a claim_id), citation_set (the [#ev:...] resolved ev-id SET), number_tokens, entity_tokens (named drugs/trials/populations/methods), polarity}. The gold COLLAPSE set = sentences sharing (claim_id AND citation_set AND polarity AND no-new-number/entity). The gold KEEP set = every sentence introducing a distinct claim_id OR a distinct number/entity OR a distinct citation_set (different basket) OR opposite polarity. For FIX-D specifically: the 6 method-paraphrases → 1 survivor, the 5 definition-paraphrases → 1 survivor, total 2; assert the two groups have DIFFERENT claim_ids (so over-merge is a labeled violation). Number/entity tokens are the ground truth for the carve-out test.
synthesis.faithfulness_guardrails:
Every candidate MUST pass these or it is DISQUALIFIED regardless of its primary score (zero-tolerance, not '95% is fine' — clinical context, lethal per §-1.1/§-1.3):

1. NEVER delete a distinct claim or a real-content span. Chrome: a real-content span (any number/entity/claim-bearing text) must never be removed (Layer-2) or withheld (render-seam). Dedup: a sentence carrying a distinct claim_id, distinct number, distinct entity, distinct citation-set, or opposite polarity must never be collapsed. One violation in the gold = DISQUALIFY.

2. CHROME = FLAG-not-DROP at the render seam (§-1.3). Chrome is WITHHELD from the rendered rollup but NEVER deleted from evidence (bibliography.json/evidence_pool.json/corpus_snapshot.json retain it). The Layer-2 clean_fetch_body filter REMOVES chrome from the body-before-span (input hygiene, allowlist-only) — that is acceptable ONLY because it is allowlist-anchored to enumerated chrome and never touches a real-content line; the render-seam screen is the FLAG-not-drop layer.

3. SAME-CITATION-SET GATE is the dedup SPINE, not a benchmarked candidate — it WRAPS whichever similarity method wins. Two sentences citing DIFFERENT ev-id sets are DIFFERENT baskets (§-1.3) and can NEVER be collapsed, even at similarity 1.0. TODAY this is keyed on the EXACT (ev_id,start,end) footprint (verified_compose.py dedup_same_span_sentences:84-140), which is precisely why offset-shifted paraphrases EVADE it — relax the grouping key to same-EV_ID-SET (ignore offsets) so paraphrases land in one group, then run the similarity collapse INSIDE the group.

4. NUMBER/ENTITY CARVE-OUT is the method-INDEPENDENT faithfulness guarantee — it OVERRIDES any similarity DROP. A flagged sentence is KEPT iff it introduces a distinct number OR named-entity token absent from every kept sibling. VERIFIED DELTA: the current carve-out (verified_compose.py _number_tokens:77-81) is NUMBER-ONLY; the task requires number/ENTITY. Extend with a deterministic entity proxy (capitalized/rare content tokens absent from the kept sibling, CONSERVATIVE: any doubt → KEEP). This is LOAD-BEARING specifically for the NLI/embedding methods, which are NUMBER-BLIND ('reduces mortality 20%' vs '35%' score ~0.99 cosine / mutually entail on structure and WOULD be wrongly collapsed without it).

5. The HARD GATES STAY FROZEN. strict_verify / NLI entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are never relaxed. All dedup runs AFTER them on the already-verified kept list (consolidate-keep-one, never a cap/thinner/target; there is no fixed N). Corroborating multi-source citations are PRESERVED via the keep-all cross-ref rewrite.

6. CONSERVATIVE BIAS on every DROP path: over-strip (chrome) and over-merge (dedup) are worse than a leak. Under-removal/under-merge is the SAFE failure direction — pick the method whose failure mode is safe (Jaccard under-merges; bidirectional-NLI requires mutual equivalence; both safe. Cosine over-merges = forbidden direction = comparison row only).
synthesis.recommended_winner_hypothesis:
HYPOTHESIS (to be confirmed by the benchmark, NOT assumed):

CHROME: a layered deterministic stack wins — (1) Layer-2 markdown/PDF post-filter in clean_fetch_body for classes 1-4 + class-5b glued-token rejoin (the ONLY fix that lands on POLARIS's current markdown input; verified: backends emit markdown, clean_fetch_body runs at the right seam), (2) the FLAG-not-drop render-seam screen with the iwire013 forensic rules ported in as the backstop for mid-prose welded chrome, (3) SymSpell+wordfreq+keep-hyphen regex as the point fix for class-5b. Layer-1 DOM extractors (rs-trafilatura/trafilatura+prune_xpath/MinerU-HTML) are the DURABLE upstream lever but are CONDITIONAL on a raw-HTML capture rebuild and are a Track-B comparison, not a drop-in winner.

DEDUP: the layered in-tree stack wins, and it is mostly ENABLE+TUNE+CONFIRM not adopt-new-tech (two of three are already built, default-off) — (1) writer-prompt constraint prevents over-generation at the source, (2) Jaccard+polarity prose pre-filter (PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE) catches near-identical restatements cheaply and deterministically with a SAFE under-merge failure mode, (3) bidirectional-NLI confirmer (PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI+PROSE) catches the divergent-verb paraphrases Jaccard misses while keeping the two distinct FIX-D claims separate. All wrapped by the same-EV_ID-SET gate + the number/ENTITY carve-out. Cosine/embedding methods LOSE on the over-merge=lethal asymmetry.

CONFIRMATION TEST that would prove/refute it: (a) chrome Track A on banked replay3 markdown shows the Layer-2 stack hits high class-1-4 removal with ZERO real-content-span loss; (b) the dedup fire-test — consolidation_nli.group_clusters on the exact 11 FIX-D sentences yields EXACTLY 2 survivors AND both groups route to the prose path (the latter already verified: '702' produces an empty FactSignature). If the fire-test yields 1 (over-merge) the NLI candidate is refuted; if it yields 11 (no collapse) the threshold needs tuning before it is the winner.
synthesis.open_questions:
1. RESOLVED by code-read (was the headline-blocking gap): does the showcased FIX-D example actually reach the prose/NLI dedup path? YES — I read extract_signature/FactSignature.is_empty (fact_dedup.py:265-303): is_empty is True unless a %-decimal, $-amount, or 19xx/20xx year is present. Bare '702' matches none, group-2 has no number, so BOTH groups yield empty signatures → prose path (which clusters intra-OR-cross-section, line 500). The 'just enable the flag' conclusion holds for the example. Still confirm on the FULL replay3 section that no FIX-D-adjacent paraphrase carries a %/$/year that would mis-route it to the numeric (cross-section-only) path.

2. CHROME raw-HTML capture seam — is the Track-B Layer-1 rebuild worth it? Crawl4AI exposes pre-markdown HTML and Jina supports 'x-respond-with: html'; capturing raw HTML upstream of clean_fetch_body would unlock rs-trafilatura/trafilatura+prune_xpath/MinerU-HTML (the durable 'chrome never enters the span' fix). Open question for the benchmark: does Track-B's gold (re-fetched, drift-caveated) show enough class-1/class-2 (affiliation) gain over the Layer-2 filter to justify a fetch-path rebuild? MinerU-HTML/GROBID are uniquely strong on class-2 author/affiliation (semantic block classification, which rules cannot do) — this is the strongest Layer-1 argument.

3. ENTITY carve-out precision: the proposed deterministic entity proxy (capitalized/rare content tokens) could miss a distinct LOWERCASE entity (a gene, a lowercase drug). Mitigation = conservative (any doubt → KEEP), but quantify the false-collapse risk on the dedup gold's entity_tokens. Is a small open-weight NER pass on the VM GPU warranted, or does the conservative heuristic suffice?

4. NLI cross-group partial-entailment risk on the shared phrase 'probability of computerisation': the bidirectional-argmax margin SHOULD prevent the method-claim and definition-claim from mutually entailing, but this is the candidate's one failure mode — the fire-test (assert 2 survivors) is the cheap converter from 'should work' to 'verified on our shape'. Tune PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI margin if it yields 1.

5. Same-EV_ID-SET gate relaxation: relaxing the dedup key from exact (ev_id,start,end) footprint to same-EV_ID-SET (ignore offsets) is required so offset-shifted paraphrases group together — but verify this does not accidentally merge two genuinely different spans of the SAME source that carry different claims (a single source cited for two distinct facts). The number/entity carve-out is the backstop, but confirm on the gold.

6. Should the Layer-2 filter and the render-seam screen share ONE rule source to avoid drift? Both target overlapping chrome classes; a single tested rule module imported by both would prevent the I-wire-013 'fix committed but blind in one of two seams' failure recurring.

candidates.name: rs-trafilatura (page-type-aware Rust extractor + XGBoost quality predictor)
candidates.year: 2026 (WCXB paper May 2026; crate posted Apr 2024, actively maintained)
candidates.approach: DOM-based, rule+ML. Three-stage page-type classifier (URL heuristics ~63%, HTML signals ~15%, XGBoost 86.6% acc) routes each page to one of 7 type-specific extraction profiles (article/forum/product/collection/listing/documentation/service), then a 27-feature XGBoost regressor predicts the result's expected F1. WCXB-verified: F1 0.859 / P 0.863 / R 0.890 / 44 ms-page — #1 on the benchmark and among the fastest.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-1 (raw-HTML only). Class 1 journal HTML furniture (JEL/Keywords/References/Cite/email-alerts/metrics): STRONG — type-aware 'documentation'/'article' profiles + the highest precision-at-recall of any rule extractor strip nav/sidebar/metrics furniture at the DOM. Class 2 affiliations: PARTIAL — author/affiliation blocks live just outside <article>; the quality predictor flags risky pages but does not target affiliation spans specifically. Class 3 paywall/preview, Class 4 cookie-consent: STRONG — boilerplate (cookie banners, footers, sidebars) is its explicit target. Classes 5/6 (glued-token, mid-word truncation): NOT addressed (it never sees the broken markdown/PDF text). HARD CONSTRAINT: POLARIS currently fetches MARKDOWN via Crawl4AI/Jina/Firecrawl, so rs-trafilatura only helps if raw HTML is captured pre-conversion (Crawl4AI exposes pre-markdown HTML; Jina supports x-respond-with: html).
candidates.integration_point: Layer-1, NEW raw-HTML capture seam on the Crawl4AI/Jina path BEFORE markdown conversion. Add as a Candidate in scripts/dr_benchmark/retrieval_bakeoff/content_extraction/_candidates.py (PyPI rs-trafilatura), bench against the incumbent on OUR 6-class fixtures, then wire into a new raw-HTML branch upstream of clean_fetch_body in src/tools/access_bypass.py.
candidates.pros: Best WCXB F1; fastest tier (44ms); page-type routing matches our heterogeneous journal/news/social mix; built-in quality predictor gives a per-source confidence weight that maps onto POLARIS's weight-not-filter DNA; Apache-licensed, self-hostable, no GPU.
candidates.cons: Rust core (Python binding is a thin wrapper — adds a non-pure-Python build dep); only helps if we capture raw HTML (the load-bearing dependency); does not touch markdown/PDF-text chrome (classes 5/6) at all; affiliation removal is incidental, not targeted.

candidates.name: Resiliparse (ChatNoir HTML2Text, recall-biased C++/Cython)
candidates.year: 2026 benchmark result; library 0.16.0 actively maintained (Webis Group)
candidates.approach: DOM-based heuristic (tag rules + regex), main_content=True flag. Built in C++/Cython for Common-Crawl-scale throughput. WCXB: F1 0.797 / P 0.783 / R 0.863 / 28 ms-page — highest recall among pure-rule extractors, fastest of all, but admits more boilerplate (precision 0.783).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-1 (raw-HTML only). Already wired in POLARIS as the recall-biased union member. Class 1 furniture: MODERATE — strips structural nav/footer but its recall bias LEAKS more metrics/cite furniture than rs-trafilatura/trafilatura (22.8% boilerplate admitted vs trafilatura 6.6%). Class 3/4 paywall+cookie: MODERATE (same recall-vs-precision tradeoff). Class 2 affiliations: WEAK (no targeting, recall bias keeps them). Classes 5/6: NOT addressed. Best used as the recall partner in a precision-gated ensemble, NOT solo — its leak rate is exactly the residual-chrome failure mode.
candidates.integration_point: ALREADY integrated: _extract_resiliparse in _candidates.py (role=candidate) and the _extract_union member. Keep as the recall leg of a union; do NOT promote to solo content-of-record given its precision profile.
candidates.pros: Fastest (28ms); pure deterministic (sovereignty-perfect); Apache-2.0; already in the harness; excellent recall for a union/consolidate strategy that matches POLARIS basket DNA.
candidates.cons: Lowest precision among the top tier — its boilerplate-admission rate IS the residual-chrome problem if used alone; only operates on raw HTML; nothing for markdown/PDF-text classes.

candidates.name: Trafilatura 2.1.0 (incumbent content-of-record) + prune_xpath
candidates.approach: DOM-based cascade of XPath queries for main content, falling back to jusText then Readability. WCXB F1 0.791 / P 0.852 / R 0.793 / 97 ms-page — best precision of the rule extractors (own eval reports up to F1 0.937 on its in-house set; that is a DIFFERENT benchmark, not WCXB). Exposes prune_xpath (str or list) to delete arbitrary subtrees BEFORE extraction and author_blacklist for author filtering.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-1 (raw-HTML only). Class 1 furniture: STRONG with default extraction + further targetable via prune_xpath (e.g. drop nodes matching //*[contains(@class,'metrics')] | //*[contains(@class,'altmetric')] | //section[@id='references']). Class 4 cookie-consent: TARGETABLE via prune_xpath against CMP dialog markup (e.g. //*[@id='onetrust-banner-sdk']). Class 2 affiliations: PARTIAL — author_blacklist filters author NAMES from metadata but does NOT remove affiliation prose from the body; needs custom prune_xpath against affiliation containers. Class 3 paywall/preview: MODERATE. Classes 5/6: NOT addressed. CRITICAL: prune_xpath needs the DOM — useless on POLARIS's current markdown input.
candidates.integration_point: ALREADY the incumbent content-of-record (safe_trafilatura_extract in access_bypass.py with SIGSEGV containment). The NEW lever is (a) capture raw HTML upstream, (b) pass a POLARIS prune_xpath list tuned to our 6 chrome classes. Tune+bench in the existing harness.
candidates.pros: Highest precision of the rule tier (matches our anti-chrome goal); prune_xpath is the single most surgical lever for OUR named classes (cookie CMP, references, metrics, affiliation containers); already the incumbent with hardened POLARIS guards; Apache-2.0, no GPU; markdown/table output modes already used.
candidates.cons: Slowest of the rule tier (97ms); prune_xpath only fires on raw HTML (the dependency); author_blacklist does not strip body affiliations; nothing for glued-token/truncation.

candidates.name: MinerU-HTML (opendatalab, Qwen3-0.6B sequence-labeling extractor)
candidates.approach: Model-based, EXTRACTIVE (not generative): a 0.6B Qwen3 LM reformulates extraction as DOM-block sequence labeling (each block = main-content vs boilerplate), with a constrained-decoding finite-state machine that bars hallucination. WCXB F1 0.827 / P 0.845 / R 0.840 / 1570 ms-page (#2 overall). On its own WebMainBench: ROUGE-N F1 0.8182 vs trafilatura 0.6358 / resiliparse 0.6233; structured-element preservation far ahead (code blocks 0.909 vs 0.131, formulas 0.940 vs 0.611).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-1 (raw-HTML only). Class 1 journal furniture: STRONGEST — semantic block classification distinguishes 'References/Cite/email-alerts/metrics' furniture from body better than any rule heuristic (its whole advantage is semantic, not positional). Class 2 affiliations: BEST of the field — a learned block classifier can label an author/affiliation block as boilerplate where rules cannot. Class 3 paywall, Class 4 cookie: STRONG. Class 6 truncation/tables: best structured-element fidelity (relevant to clinical PDF→HTML tables). Classes 5 (glued-token): NOT addressed (operates on HTML blocks, not broken text). Faithfulness-safe: extractive + FSM-constrained = no paraphrase, so it cannot fabricate a span.
candidates.integration_point: Layer-1 clinical/table lane, VM GPU only. ALREADY registered (_extract_mineru_html, role=candidate, needs_gpu=True, extractive=True) and GPU-gated in run_bakeoff.py. Matches the memory pin (clinical-PDF=mineru25). Run on the VM's local 2xRTX3090Ti per the sovereignty/local-GPU rule.
candidates.pros: Best semantic boilerplate discrimination (the exact failure mode of classes 1-2 that rules miss); extractive + FSM-constrained = faithfulness-safe (no fabrication); Apache-2.0 + open weights (sovereignty-compatible on local GPU); far superior table/formula fidelity for clinical PDFs; already in the harness.
candidates.cons: 1570 ms-page — ~16-56x slower than rule extractors (run-speed cost); needs GPU (VM-only, not the laptop per §8.4); still raw-HTML-only — does nothing for markdown/PDF-text classes 5; 0.6B model adds VRAM + serving complexity.

candidates.name: jusText (stoplist-density boilerplate classifier)
candidates.approach: DOM-based; classifies paragraphs as good/bad/short/near-good using link density + a language-specific stoplist of common function words. WCXB F1 0.707 / P 0.771 / R 0.695. POLARIS sets the English stoplist explicitly (its published 0.478 was a multilingual mis-set artifact).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-1 (raw-HTML only). Class 1 furniture: MODERATE — stoplist density flags low-text-density nav/cite/metrics blocks well, but short furniture lines ('ISSN:', 'Crossref: 53') can pass as 'short' rather than 'bad'. Class 4 cookie/Class 3 paywall: MODERATE. Class 2 affiliations: WEAK (affiliation prose has normal word density). DROPS TABLES entirely (bad for clinical). Classes 5/6: NOT addressed. Value is purely as the 3rd union member (already wired), not solo.
candidates.integration_point: ALREADY integrated as the 3rd _extract_union member with the English stoplist correction. Keep as a union member only.
candidates.pros: Pure deterministic; language-aware; useful diversity in a union/consolidate strategy; already correctly configured in the harness.
candidates.cons: Mid-pack F1; drops tables (clinical-hostile); weak on short furniture lines (exactly our ISSN/Crossref/metrics class); raw-HTML only.

candidates.name: Mozilla Readability / readability-lxml (last-resort fallback)
candidates.year: readability-lxml 0.8.4.1 (pre-2024 incumbent); Mozilla Readability JS actively maintained; WCXB 2026
candidates.approach:
DOM-based scoring of

/

by text/link/comma density (the original Readability algorithm; Mozilla Readability is the Firefox Reader-View JS port). WCXB: Readability F1 0.674 / P 0.684 / R 0.712 / 785 ms-page — bottom tier.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-1 (raw-HTML only). Class 1 furniture: WEAK-MODERATE — density scoring keeps too much sidebar/related content (low precision 0.684). Class 2/3/4: WEAK. Classes 5/6: NOT addressed. Only justified as trafilatura's internal final fallback (which trafilatura already invokes).
candidates.integration_point: ALREADY registered (_extract_readability, role=baseline) and is trafilatura's own last-resort fallback. No standalone action — keep as the floor baseline only.
candidates.pros: Ubiquitous, deterministic, zero-config; already trafilatura's internal fallback; Mozilla JS variant is battle-tested in Firefox Reader View.
candidates.cons: Lowest precision/F1 of the field; keeps related-content furniture (our class-1 failure mode); slow (785ms) for its tier; raw-HTML only.

candidates.name: POLARIS markdown/PDF-text post-filter (deterministic block-classifier + glued-token rejoin) — Layer 2
candidates.approach: Deterministic post-extraction filter operating on POLARIS's ACTUAL current input (Jina/Crawl4AI markdown + PDF text), since the three live fetch backends all emit markdown — DOM extractors cannot see this. Extends the existing allowlist clean_fetch_body / strip_web_boilerplate with: (a) markdown-line block rules for the named class-1 furniture literals (JEL Classification / Keywords / View All Journal Metrics / Associated Records / References Biographies Cite / Receive email alerts / Download to reference manager / ISSN:/Crossref:/Web of Science: counters / Published Time:); (b) class-2 affiliation/disclosure line patterns; (c) class-3 paywall-preview markers (Member-only story / What you'll learn:); (d) class-4 cookie-consent line; (e) class-5b glued-token-with-delimiter rejoin ('Governan; ce'->'Governance') via a deterministic dictionary check (NOT a hyphen artifact — dehyphen won't catch it).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-2 (works on CURRENT markdown/PDF input — the ONLY layer that helps without a raw-HTML capture rebuild). Classes 1-4: directly targetable as anchored whole-line / line-prefix markdown rules (allowlist-only, faithfulness-neutral, FLAG-not-delete per the chrome predicate in weighted_enrichment.py). Class 5a line-wrap hyphen: ALREADY handled by _LINE_WRAP_HYPHEN_RE. Class 5b glued-token-with-delimiter: NEW deterministic dictionary-rejoin. Class 6 mid-word truncation: out of scope per task (fresh-fetch + span-snap handles it; note it explicitly).
candidates.integration_point: Layer-2, IN access_bypass.py clean_fetch_body (called from live_retriever.py _build_provenance_quote at line 2135-2137 BEFORE the span is built) + the shared chrome predicate is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable in weighted_enrichment.py. This is the only fix that lands on the input POLARIS produces TODAY.
candidates.pros: Operates on the ACTUAL current markdown/PDF input (no raw-HTML rebuild required); deterministic + fast (sovereignty-perfect, run-speed-perfect); extends the existing proven seam; allowlist-only/flag-not-delete = faithfulness-safe per §-1.3; the glued-token dictionary-rejoin is the correct fix for class 5b where dehyphen fails.
candidates.cons: Allowlist of literals is maintenance-heavy and recall-bounded (only catches enumerated furniture, not novel chrome); does not get the structural DOM-level removal that a raw-HTML extractor would; needs the §-1.1 forensic detector as its yardstick to find new furniture classes.

candidates.name: dehyphen (Flair char-LM perplexity de-hyphenation) — comparison row, NOT a pick
candidates.approach: Model-based: reconstructs continuous text from PDF-broken lines by scoring join candidates with Flair character-based LM perplexity; picks the lowest-perplexity join. Primarily German; multilingual fallback model.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Class 5a line-wrap hyphen ONLY — and POLARIS already solves this deterministically with _LINE_WRAP_HYPHEN_RE. It does NOT address class 5b glued-token-with-delimiter ('Governan; ce') because that is a delimiter-insertion artifact, not a hyphen. Adds no coverage over the existing deterministic fix.
candidates.integration_point: NONE recommended. Listed only as the model-based comparison row for de-hyphenation so the ranking is honest.
candidates.pros: Probabilistic join can resolve genuinely ambiguous PDF wraps that a pure rule misses.
candidates.cons: German-biased; loads a Flair char-LM (model weight + latency, against the deterministic/fast preference); does NOT solve class 5b; redundant with the existing _LINE_WRAP_HYPHEN_RE for class 5a; low maintenance. Not sovereignty-aligned as a core dep.

candidates.name: ReaderLM-v2 (Jina, generative HTML->markdown SLM) — yardstick only
candidates.approach: GENERATIVE 1.5B model that rewrites HTML into clean markdown. WCXB F1 0.741 / P 0.741 / R 0.790 / 10410 ms-page (slowest by far).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Layer-1. Would aggressively remove all chrome classes 1-4 cleanly, BUT it is GENERATIVE — it can paraphrase/fabricate, violating POLARIS's faithfulness law (never alter a verbatim span). Structurally barred from being content-of-record.
candidates.integration_point: ALREADY registered as role=yardstick_non_sovereign, extractive=False, structurally barred from winning (is_eligible_to_win). Reference comparison only.
candidates.pros: Cleanest-looking chrome removal; good for an upper-bound reference.
candidates.cons: GENERATIVE = fabrication risk = disqualified as content-of-record (faithfulness law); CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial — sovereignty-incompatible for the product); 10.4 s-page (catastrophically slow); needs GPU. Yardstick only, never crowned.

recommended_shortlist: Layer-2 deterministic markdown/PDF-text post-filter in clean_fetch_body (THE fix that lands on POLARIS's CURRENT input — Jina/Crawl4AI/Firecrawl all emit markdown, so this is the only layer that helps without a raw-HTML rebuild; covers classes 1-4 + class-5b glued-token rejoin; faithfulness-safe flag-not-delete)

rs-trafilatura (the durable upstream Layer-1 fix IF raw-HTML capture is added: #1 WCXB, page-type-aware, fastest, built-in quality predictor = per-source weight; pairs with a NEW raw-HTML capture seam on the Crawl4AI/Jina path)
Trafilatura 2.1.0 + POLARIS-tuned prune_xpath (incumbent; the most surgical Layer-1 lever for our NAMED classes — cookie CMP, references, metrics, affiliation containers — but only fires on raw HTML)

MinerU-HTML on VM GPU for the clinical/table lane (best SEMANTIC boilerplate discrimination = the exact classes 1-2 rules miss; extractive + FSM-constrained = faithfulness-safe; already pinned as clinical-PDF winner)
research.notes:
LOAD-BEARING ARCHITECTURAL FINDING (verified by grep of src/tools/access_bypass.py lines 13-15 + live_retriever.py line 2131-2159): POLARIS's three live fetch backends — Crawl4AI, Jina Reader, Firecrawl — ALL emit MARKDOWN, not raw HTML. Two of the task's residual-chrome samples ('Markdown Content: ## 1. Introduction', 'Published Time: 2024-09-04') are literal Jina Reader markdown-preamble signatures, proving the offending spans enter through the markdown-reader path. Therefore the candidate field splits into TWO layers that fix DIFFERENT places, and they MUST NOT be ranked on one axis:

- LAYER 1 (durable 'chrome never enters the span', DOM-based): rs-trafilatura / trafilatura+prune_xpath / resiliparse / MinerU-HTML / jusText / readability. Every one of these needs RAW HTML and DOM structure. They are USELESS on POLARIS's current markdown input. They only become available if POLARIS adds a raw-HTML capture seam BEFORE markdown conversion (Crawl4AI already has pre-markdown HTML; Jina supports the 'x-respond-with: html' header). This is the advisor's recommended durable fix and the single biggest lever — but it is a fetch-path rebuild, not a drop-in.
- LAYER 2 (works on POLARIS's ACTUAL CURRENT input): the deterministic markdown/PDF-text post-filter extending clean_fetch_body. This is the ONLY layer that improves things without the raw-HTML rebuild, and is the fastest path to killing classes 1-4 today.

RANKING-AXIS WARNING (per CLAUDE.md §-1.1/§-1.3, advisor-confirmed): do NOT rank these by WCXB F1 (0.859 > 0.827 > 0.797...). WCXB measures GENERAL main-content extraction on RAW HTML across 7 page types — not our 6 chrome classes, and not on our markdown/PDF input. Ranking by the benchmark number is exactly the banned 'metric-as-quality' pattern. Rank by handles_our_shapes (does it kill OUR journal-furniture / affiliation / paywall / cookie / glued-token classes on OUR input); WCXB F1 is a secondary reference column only.

DE-HYPHENATION PRECISION (class 5 is two sub-classes): 5a line-wrap hyphen ('high- risk', 'worker- level') is ALREADY solved deterministically by _LINE_WRAP_HYPHEN_RE in access_bypass.py — no new tool needed. 5b glued-token-with-delimiter ('Governan; ce', 'Agricultural Oc; b') is NOT a hyphen artifact, so dehyphen (Flair char-LM, German-biased, model-based) will NOT catch it; the correct fix is a deterministic dictionary-rejoin (Layer-2). dehyphen is listed only as the honest model comparison row, not a pick.

CLASS 6 (mid-word truncation 'usand workers') is per the task itself a BANKED-corpus artifact resolved by fresh-fetch + span-snap; it is out of scope for extraction libraries — noted, not assigned a tool.

HARNESS REALITY: POLARIS ALREADY has a content-extraction bake-off harness at scripts/dr_benchmark/retrieval_bakeoff/content_extraction/ with trafilatura(incumbent)/resiliparse/justext/readability/union/MinerU-HTML/ReaderLM-v2 registered (ids web-verified 2026-06-23, role-gated so the generative yardstick can never be crowned, GPU-gated for the model candidates). The integration work is: (1) ship the Layer-2 markdown/PDF post-filter NOW (drop-in, in clean_fetch_body); (2) add rs-trafilatura to the registry + add a raw-HTML capture seam, then bench Layer-1 candidates on OUR 6-class fixtures (NOT WCXB) using the existing §-1.1 forensic detector as the yardstick.

SOVEREIGNTY: rs-trafilatura/trafilatura/resiliparse/jusText/readability = open + self-hostable, deterministic (rs-trafilatura is hybrid rule+ML, still local/no-GPU). MinerU-HTML = open-weight, model-but-EXTRACTIVE + FSM-constrained (faithfulness-safe), VM-GPU only. ReaderLM-v2 = GENERATIVE (fabrication risk, disqualified as content-of-record) + CC-BY-NC (non-commercial, sovereignty-incompatible) = yardstick only. dehyphen = model, German-biased, not core-aligned.

FAITHFULNESS NOTE: any Layer-2 line removal must be FLAG-not-delete per §-1.3 (chrome withheld from the rendered rollup, never deleted from evidence) and must keep the number/entity carve-out; the strict_verify/NLI/4-role/span-grounding gates stay frozen.

candidates.name: Trafilatura (HTML boilerplate/content extractor)
candidates.approach: Deterministic/heuristic HTML-to-text extractor (hybrid of jusText + readability algorithms + its own XPath/DOM rules). Strips recurring noise: headers, footers, navigation, sidebars, comment widgets; isolates the main article node. Also extracts metadata (title/author/date/sitename) separately, so masthead furniture is partitioned away from body. No model, no external API, no DB.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: HITS class 1 (nav/journal/site HTML chrome: 'View All Journal Metrics','Associated Records','Download to reference manager','Receive email alerts'), class 4 (cookie/consent banners), and much of class 3 (paywall/preview wrappers removed as boilerplate when the body node survives). Does NOT touch class 5 glued-token/de-hyphenation, class 6 mid-word truncation, or author/affiliation prose that is INSIDE the body node (class 2). Caveat: only helps if applied at FETCH time on raw HTML; cannot repair chrome already welded into stored spans.
candidates.integration_point: src/tools/access_bypass.clean_fetch_body — run as the primary HTML->text pass on raw fetched HTML before _build_provenance_quote stores spans; this is the durable upstream fix.
candidates.pros: Single fetch-time pass kills 3 of our 6 residual classes at the source; Apache-2.0; pure-Python, fast, no GPU; actively maintained (Jun 2026); already metadata-aware so masthead is separated not deleted. Most-cited modern boilerplate extractor with strong benchmark recall.
candidates.cons: HTML-only (does nothing for PDF-sourced spans); body-internal author/affiliation prose and intra-word glue survive; operates on raw HTML so it must be wired at fetch time, not as a render screen; can occasionally over-trim short main-content pages.

candidates.name: GROBID (scholarly TEI structurer)
candidates.approach: Self-hosted service (Docker) using CRF + optional Deep Learning (DeLFT: RNN/transformers) to parse a scholarly PDF into structured TEI: dedicated header model (title/abstract/authors/affiliations/keywords), separate body model (paragraphs/sections/callouts), and reference model. Author and affiliation/address blocks are parsed into discrete fields, structurally separating them from body prose. De-hyphenation of line-broken words is done natively in body text via TextUtilities.dehyphenize() in TEIFormatter (verified in repo issue #180 + GROBID docs).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: HITS class 2 (author/affiliation/masthead furniture: 'Juan Duran-Vanegas and Dora Tuda are affiliated with...','Principal investigator:','Conflict of Interest Disclosure:' land in TEI header/note fields, OUT of body), class 5 line-break hyphenation ('finan-\nciellen'-style trailing-hyphen joins), and class 1 reference/metadata furniture (refs go to a separate TEI list, not body). Does NOT fix class 5 intra-word SEMICOLON/space glue ('Governan; ce','high- risk' kept-hyphen case) — its dehyphenize targets line-end hyphens only. Does NOT apply to HTML web pages (PDF/scholarly input only) so class 3/4 web chrome is out of scope.
candidates.integration_point: A fetch/ingest pre-pass for scholarly PDF sources feeding access_bypass / live_retriever — use GROBID's structured TEI body as the evidence text so affiliation/refs furniture never enters spans; pairs with the locked MinerU clinical-PDF path.
candidates.pros: Best-in-class structural separation of author/affiliation/refs from body — kills class 2 at the source the way no regex can; native line-break de-hyphenation; Apache-2.0, Dockerized, fully self-hostable; mature and actively released (Apr 2026). One tool covers classes 2 + 5(line-break) + 1(refs).
candidates.cons: Scholarly-PDF-scoped — useless for HTML web chrome (classes 3/4) and for non-paper sources (news, blogs, gov pages); requires running a JVM service (heavier than a library); DL models want a GPU for best accuracy; does not repair intra-word semicolon-glue.

candidates.name: SymSpell (symspellpy) word-segmentation/compound repair + wordfreq dictionary validation
candidates.year: symspellpy 6.9.0 (2025-03-09, MIT); wordfreq 3.1.1 (2023-11-21, Apache-2.0, stable validator utility)
candidates.approach: Deterministic. SymSpell WordSegmentation / LookupCompound explicitly fix 'mistakenly inserted spaces within a correct word' and 'mistakenly omitted spaces' against a frequency dictionary (Symmetric Delete algorithm, offline). wordfreq gives an offline word-frequency lookup to validate that a candidate merge ('Governan'+'ce' -> 'Governance') is a real word. RECOMMENDED PAIR: a deterministic regex rejoin for the kept-hyphen case ((\w+)-\s+(\w+) -> close the space, KEEP hyphen) PLUS SymSpell/wordfreq for the spurious-glue case (drop junk char, close space, dictionary-validate). The regex must run first so legit compounds are not over-merged.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: HITS class 5 specifically: 'high- risk'/'worker- level' -> regex rejoin to 'high-risk'/'worker-level' (keep hyphen); 'Governan; ce','Agricultural Oc; b Polarization' -> strip the spurious '; ' and dictionary-validate the closed token. Partially aids class 6 (a truncated 'usand workers' can be dictionary-checked but NOT reconstructed — flag only). Does NOT touch chrome classes 1-4.
candidates.integration_point: src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever._build_provenance_quote (and a shared text-normalize helper called from access_bypass.clean_fetch_body) — token-repair as a normalization pass before spans are stored, with wordfreq as the merge-validator.
candidates.pros: The honest right answer for class 5 intra-word glue that GROBID/standard de-hyphenation MISS (they only handle trailing -\n); fully offline, MIT/Apache, no GPU; dictionary-validated so a faithful real token is never corrupted; fast.
candidates.cons: SymSpell WordSegmentation REMOVES spaces, so used naively it over-merges legit hyphenated compounds ('high- risk' -> 'highrisk') — must be gated behind the keep-hyphen regex and dictionary check; wordfreq is 2023 (pre-2024) so cited as a stable incumbent validator utility, not a frontier claim; cannot reconstruct a genuinely cut word (class 6).

candidates.name: MinerU (locked clinical-PDF layout parser) — native furniture removal
candidates.year: 2026 (v3.4.0, 2026-06-18; v2.5 'MinerU2.5-Pro' is the POLARIS-locked pick, same pipeline lineage; AGPL/Apache per release — verify the exact tag you run)
candidates.approach: Layout-aware document parser (open-weight models + pipeline). README explicitly: 'Remove headers, footers, footnotes, page numbers, etc., to ensure semantic coherence' and 'Output text in human-readable order' + preserve heading/paragraph/list structure. So page furniture is dropped structurally and reading order is restored.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: HITS class 1 page furniture from PDF sources (running headers/footers, page numbers, footnote rows like 'Web of Science: 49','Crossref: 53' when they are footer/metadata blocks) and improves class 6 by restoring reading order (reduces span-cut artifacts). Author/affiliation (class 2) and de-hyphenation (class 5) are NOT explicitly claimed in the README — capability INFERRED from layout labelling, not confirmed at primary source. No effect on HTML web chrome (classes 3/4).
candidates.integration_point: Already the locked clinical-PDF ingest path; ensure its header/footer/page-number stripping output is what feeds spans (verify the running clinical path actually consumes the cleaned body, not raw OCR). Confirm de-hyphenation behavior on the 2.5 tag you actually run.
candidates.pros: Already in the locked stack — zero new dependency; verified native header/footer/page-number/footnote removal addresses class 1 for clinical PDFs; reading-order restoration reduces truncation artifacts; open-weight, runs on the VM GPU.
candidates.cons: Author/affiliation + de-hyphenation capability UNVERIFIED at primary source (do not claim handles class 2/5 without confirming on your 2.5 tag); version drift to confirm (memory=2.5 locked vs research=3.4.0); model step (GPU); PDF-only — no HTML chrome coverage; verify license tag in use for sovereignty.

candidates.name: EasyList Cookie List (deterministic consent filter-list)
candidates.approach: Community-maintained Adblock-format filter list whose Cookie List specifically targets cookie-consent / GDPR banner elements. Primarily DOM-selector rules (intended for uBlock/ABP); the text signatures and selectors can be mined into a deterministic phrase/selector denylist for cookie banners.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: HITS class 4 (cookie/consent: 'The content references language or the region that you are in', generic consent text) and reinforces class 1 consent-adjacent nav. Best used as a DOM filter at fetch time alongside Trafilatura; as a raw text-phrase denylist it needs adaptation (the rules are selector-oriented, not free-text). No effect on classes 2/3/5/6.
candidates.integration_point: Fetch-time DOM filtering before HTML->text in access_bypass.clean_fetch_body, OR mine its consent strings into the existing _WEB_CHROME_MARKERS denylist.
candidates.pros: Frontier-current (continuously updated), community-maintained, broad consent coverage; deterministic; complements Trafilatura for the residual cookie banners it misses.
candidates.cons: Selector-oriented (DOM), so not a drop-in free-text matcher — needs adaptation/mining for our span-text path; GPL/CC-BY-SA attribution obligations; narrow (cookie/consent only); overlaps with what Trafilatura already removes.

candidates.name: Deterministic FLAG-not-drop render-seam screen (POLARIS design, class (e))
candidates.year: 2026 (in-tree design; port from scripts/iwire013_sec11_forensic_audit.py detector, I-wire-013 #1327)
candidates.approach: Deterministic last-line backstop, NOT a third-party tool. One shared predicate over every claim-bearing render unit that WITHHOLDS chrome/truncated units from the rendered rollup but NEVER deletes them from evidence (§-1.3 clinical-safety FLAG-not-drop). Rules ported from the independent forensic detector that already reproduces 55 chrome / 20 truncation on banked renders where the production predicate reported ~0 (CONTAINMENT rules: glued inline headers, affiliation-middot lists, dotted-ToC tokens, bare-DOI/ORCID rows, masthead/license/biblio regexes) + corpus-grounded truncation via is_truncated_fragment.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Backstop for ALL 6 classes that survive extraction: glued mid-prose chrome (classes 1/2), residual cookie/paywall fragments (3/4), and corpus-grounded truncation (6). It is the SAFETY NET, not the primary fix — the advisor's point stands that a render screen 'can't hit zero', so it complements extraction rather than replacing it. Class 5 glue is better repaired upstream than flagged here.
candidates.integration_point: src/polaris_graph/generator/weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable + the render-seam chokepoint (single pass over every claim unit, FLAG-not-drop, feeds the chrome-as-claim canary).
candidates.pros: Fully sovereign, deterministic, fast; FLAG-not-drop preserves faithfulness law (never deletes a real span); CONTAINMENT rules catch chrome welded mid-paragraph that whole-unit classifiers miss; already has a fail-loud canary to prevent regressions shipping.
candidates.cons: Cannot hit zero (recall-bounded backstop); precision/recall tension on a DROP path (over-strip risk is worse than a leak per §-1.3); does not repair anything (suppress-only), so de-hyphenation/glue must be fixed upstream; rule maintenance burden.

candidates.name: Resiliparse extract_plain_text (fast rule-based main-content) — comparison/alt to Trafilatura
candidates.approach: Deterministic, very fast rule-based main-content extraction. main_content=True removes 'navigation blocks, sidebars, footers, some ads, and (as far as detectable without rendering) invisible elements'. Built for web-archive scale.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: HITS class 1 (nav/footer chrome) and part of class 4; faster than Trafilatura at corpus scale. Docs do NOT specifically target cookie banners/mastheads, and it has weaker metadata partitioning than Trafilatura, so class 2/3 coverage is thinner. No class 5/6.
candidates.integration_point: Drop-in alternative to Trafilatura at access_bypass.clean_fetch_body where throughput dominates accuracy.
candidates.pros: Extremely fast (C++ core), Apache-2.0, deterministic, web-archive grade; good when fetch volume is huge.
candidates.cons: Lower extraction precision and weaker metadata/masthead separation than Trafilatura; no explicit cookie/paywall targeting; overlaps Trafilatura — pick one as primary.

candidates.name: Docling (layout-aware document parser) — comparison row
candidates.approach: MIT layout-aware parser (PDF/DOCX/HTML -> unified doc model with page layout, reading order, table structure). Self-hostable / air-gapped capable.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: PLAUSIBLY aids class 1 furniture + class 2 via layout labelling and class 5 via reading-order normalization, BUT the README/PyPI do NOT explicitly state header/footer/page-furniture separation, author/affiliation labelling, or de-hyphenation — capability INFERRED, not confirmed. candidates.name: Same-citation-set / span-footprint gate (deterministic prefilter — POLARIS-native, extends dedup_same_span_sentences)
candidates.approach: Deterministic. Group composed sentences by the SET of citation tokens / resolved (ev_id,start,end) provenance they carry. This is NOT itself a similarity method — it is the faithfulness GATE that must run FIRST: only sentences citing the SAME source set are even eligible to be collapsed. A text-similarity collapse (Jaccard / TF-IDF / embedding cosine) then runs WITHIN each same-citation group.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP: the POLARIS sample paraphrases all cite the SAME [8][9][10] -> same citation group -> eligible. KEEP (structural): two sentences citing DIFFERENT sources are DIFFERENT baskets (§-1.3) and are NEVER collapsed, even if textually near-identical. This is the single most faithful primary filter and it is half-built today (the footprint key already exists). It does NOT separate same-claim-reword from distinct-number-same-source on its own — that is the carve-out's job (see notes).
candidates.integration_point: Already the spine of verified_compose._compose_section_per_basket (seen_numbers_by_footprint, idx8 seen-span). Today it requires footprint EQUALITY; the gap is that paraphrases anchor to slightly different offsets so footprints differ. Fix: relax the grouping key from exact (ev_id,start,end) footprint to same EV_ID-SET (ignore offsets) to form the candidate group, then run a text-similarity collapse inside the group.
candidates.pros: Free (tokens already on each sentence), order-preserving, zero approximation error, enforces §-1.3 basket faithfulness by construction (cross-basket collapse is structurally impossible). Surgical, not a rewrite.
candidates.cons: Not a similarity detector by itself — must be paired with a within-group text-similarity collapse + the number/entity carve-out. Misses cross-basket exact dupes (correct behavior: those are distinct baskets and must stay).

candidates.name: Number/entity carve-out (the method-INDEPENDENT faithfulness guarantee — extend POLARIS _number_tokens)
candidates.year: 2026 (in-tree, number-only today; entity extension is the delta vs the ask)
candidates.approach: Deterministic content-token guard applied AFTER any similarity method flags a pair as near-duplicate. A flagged sentence is KEPT (overriding the DROP) iff it introduces a distinct number token (or distinct named-entity token) absent from every already-kept sibling in its group. A pure reword (no new number/entity) collapses.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: This is the row that actually answers the task's KEEP requirement. DROP: '...employed a novel methodology to estimate probability of computerisation for 702 occupations' x6 — same number set {702}, no new token -> reword -> collapse to one. KEEP: 'reduces mortality 20%' vs '...35%' — distinct number {20} vs {35} -> both kept. NO similarity method does this separation alone; the carve-out is the faithfulness guarantee regardless of which similarity primitive is chosen.
candidates.integration_point: Wrap whichever collapse method is chosen: similarity_flags_drop AND NOT (new_number_tokens OR new_entity_tokens) -> drop; else keep. Current code is NUMBER-ONLY; the task asks number/ENTITY. Extend with a deterministic entity proxy (capitalized / rare content tokens absent from the kept sibling) so a distinct drug/trial/population name is never dropped.
candidates.pros: Directly enforces FAITHFULNESS LAW (a number/entity-bearing sentence is never dropped). Method-agnostic — bolts onto Jaccard, TF-IDF, or embedding cosine identically. Already exists for numbers.
candidates.cons: Entity proxy is heuristic (capitalization/rarity); a lowercase distinct entity could slip. Mitigate by being conservative (any doubt -> KEEP).

candidates.name: n-gram (word-shingle) Jaccard — exact, within same-citation group
candidates.year: classic (incumbent baseline; permitted comparison/core per mandate)
candidates.primary_source_url: https://ekzhu.com/datasketch/minhash.html (Jaccard definition + exact reference); shingle method in ContentDeduplicator
candidates.approach: Deterministic. Tokenize each sentence into word k-shingles (k=3 per POLARIS DEFAULT_SHINGLE_SIZE), compute EXACT Jaccard = |A∩B|/|A∪B| between sentence shingle sets. At within-section scale (tens of sentences) exact Jaccard is trivial — no MinHash approximation needed.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP: heavy reword with synonym swaps ('study introduced' vs 'authors developed' vs 'investigation employed' + identical tail) keeps high 3-gram overlap on the shared tail -> high Jaccard -> flagged, then collapsed (passes carve-out: no new number). KEEP: distinct-number sentence shares most shingles BUT the carve-out overrides the drop. KEEP (lexical): genuinely distinct claim with low shingle overlap -> low Jaccard -> never flagged.
candidates.integration_point: Run inside the same-citation group as the collapse step. Threshold: word-3-gram Jaccard >= ~0.6-0.7 for reword-DROP candidacy (POLARIS ContentDeduplicator near_duplicate_threshold is 0.85 on MinHash-estimated Jaccard, but that is char/word-shingle MinHash at doc scale; for sentence rewords with synonym swap use a lower lexical bar ~0.6 and lean on the carve-out for safety).
candidates.pros: Exact, fast at our scale, fully sovereign/deterministic, interpretable threshold. No model, no GPU. Strong on the POLARIS sample (shared identical tail clause).
candidates.cons: Pure synonym-only paraphrase with NO shared n-grams evades it (lexical, not semantic). Threshold tuning is corpus-sensitive. Caught only by the embedding-cosine upgrade row.

candidates.name: TF-IDF cosine — within same-citation group
candidates.year: classic (incumbent baseline; permitted core per mandate)
candidates.approach: Deterministic. Vectorize the candidate group's sentences with TF-IDF (fit on the group/section), compute pairwise cosine. Down-weights high-frequency function words and up-weights the distinctive content terms a reword shares, so it separates same-claim from distinct-claim better than raw Jaccard on short text.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP: rewords share the high-IDF content terms ('computerisation','702','Gaussian process classifier') -> high TF-IDF cosine -> flagged -> collapse (carve-out: no new number). KEEP: a distinct claim shares only common terms -> low cosine (distinctive terms differ). KEEP: distinct-number reword -> high cosine BUT carve-out overrides (TF-IDF, like all bag-of-words, treats '20%' and '35%' as different tokens, which actually HELPS slightly, but do not rely on it — the carve-out is the guarantee).
candidates.integration_point: sklearn TfidfVectorizer over the same-citation group, cosine >= ~0.8 -> reword candidate, then carve-out. Pairs naturally with the deterministic core (it is the recommended sovereign collapse primitive alongside n-gram Jaccard).
candidates.pros: Better short-text discrimination than raw Jaccard (IDF weighting), still deterministic/sovereign/CPU, no GPU. Robust to function-word filler differences. Mature, well-understood threshold.
candidates.cons: Still lexical — fully synonym-swapped paraphrase with no shared content terms evades it. IDF must be fit per group/section (small-sample IDF is noisy). Marked unverified: the URL is the canonical sklearn API but I did not fetch it this session.

candidates.name: Sentence-embedding cosine — Qwen3-Embedding (0.6B/4B/8B) on the VM GPU
candidates.approach: Open-weight neural. Embed each sentence in the candidate group, compute cosine. Captures SEMANTIC near-duplication — collapses fully synonym-swapped paraphrases that share NO n-grams. This is the only method on the list that catches pure-synonym rewords.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP: 'The exposure measure chosen for the analysis was the probability of computerisation' vs 'The probability of computerisation was the exposure measure adopted' -> near-zero lexical overlap on word order but cosine ~0.97 -> flagged -> collapse (carve-out: no new number). LETHAL TRAP / KEEP: embeddings are NUMBER-BLIND — 'reduces mortality 20%' vs '...35%' score ~0.99 cosine and WOULD be wrongly collapsed. The number/entity carve-out is therefore MANDATORY and load-bearing for this method specifically; it is the only thing preventing a distinct-statistic faithfulness violation.
candidates.integration_point: Run on the VM's local GPU (open-weight, Apache-2.0) only when justified by paraphrase volume; 0.6B suffices at sentence scale. Cosine >= ~0.93 (== NeMo eps 0.07) for an aggressive collapse, ~0.97 (== eps 0.03) for conservative; ALWAYS gated by same-citation-set AND the number/entity carve-out. POLARIS already has the PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL=qwen3 hook in src/utils/embedding_service.py.
candidates.pros: Only method that catches pure-synonym paraphrase (the residual the lexical methods miss). Open-weight, self-hostable, runs on the VM GPU per the sovereignty constraint. 2025 frontier-verified. Multilingual (matches POLARIS multilingual evidence).
candidates.cons: NUMBER/ENTITY-BLIND -> WILL false-drop distinct statistics without the carve-out (the lethal trap, must be flagged). Non-deterministic across hardware/precision (mitigate: fixed model+dtype, threshold margin). GPU cost + run-time. Threshold corpus-sensitive. Use as an OPTIONAL semantic-recall upgrade layered on the deterministic core, not the primary.

candidates.name: NeMo Curator semantic deduplication (SemDeDup-lineage: embed -> k-means cluster -> intra-cluster cosine)
candidates.approach: Open-weight neural pipeline: embed all items, k-means cluster to bound pairwise cost, compute intra-cluster cosine, keep ONE representative per cluster within eps. This is the production framing of embedding-cosine dedup at SCALE (millions of docs).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP/KEEP semantics identical to the Qwen3 embedding-cosine row (it IS embedding cosine + clustering). Default eps=0.01 (cosine_sim ~0.99, strict), quick-start eps=0.07 (cosine_sim ~0.93), which_to_keep hard/easy/random picks the survivor. Same number-blind trap -> same carve-out requirement. KEEP across clusters is automatic but is NOT the citation-set guarantee POLARIS needs.
candidates.integration_point: NOT a fit as-is: the k-means clustering layer is for web-scale corpora; POLARIS dedup runs on tens of sentences within a section, so clustering is pure overhead. Borrow only the threshold guidance (eps 0.01-0.1 -> cosine 0.99-0.90) and which_to_keep policy; default embedder is google/embeddinggemma-300m (closed-weight-adjacent, prefer Qwen3 for sovereignty).
candidates.pros: Authoritative 2025 production reference that satisfies the frontier date floor for the embedding-cosine recommendation; concrete, comparable eps thresholds; survivor-selection policy.
candidates.cons: Clustering is over-engineering at within-section scale (adds latency + a k hyperparameter for no benefit on tens of sentences). Default embedder not the sovereign pick. Same number/entity-blind trap. Cite for thresholds + provenance, do not adopt the clustering machinery.

candidates.name: MinHash + LSH (datasketch) — already in-tree (ContentDeduplicator)
candidates.approach: Probabilistic. Approximate Jaccard via MinHash signatures (128 hashes default), use LSH for sub-linear candidate retrieval at scale. POLARIS already ships a hand-rolled MinHash+SimHash ContentDeduplicator (near_duplicate_threshold 0.85).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP/KEEP semantics are APPROXIMATE Jaccard -> same lexical separation as exact n-gram Jaccard (catches shared-tail rewords; misses pure-synonym). The approximation buys nothing here: at tens-of-sentences scale, exact Jaccard is trivial and has ZERO false positives/negatives, whereas MinHash/LSH inject approximation error (datasketch docs explicitly note false positives/negatives) for a sub-linear win that only matters at millions of items.
candidates.integration_point: Comparison row only. If reused, call ContentDeduplicator.calculate_similarity per same-citation pair — but prefer exact Jaccard at this scale. Note its 0.85 is MinHash-ESTIMATED Jaccard (a sampling estimate of the same metric), not a different similarity.
candidates.pros: Already in the codebase (seeded, reproducible). Battle-tested at web scale. Sovereign.
candidates.cons: Approximation error for no scale benefit at within-section size; do NOT over-rank just because it is imported. Lexical only (synonym-swap blind). Rank below exact Jaccard/TF-IDF for this use.

candidates.name: SimHash (Charikar 2002; Manku et al. web-crawl) — in-tree fingerprint
candidates.primary_source_url: https://research.google.com/pubs/archive/33026.pdf (Manku, Jain, Das Sarma — Detecting Near-Duplicates for Web Crawling)
candidates.approach: Probabilistic. 64-bit fingerprint from weighted token hashes; near-duplicate iff Hamming distance <= 3. Designed for web-scale crawl dedup of long documents.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP: long, mostly-identical documents. KEEP: low-overlap documents. KNOWN-WEAK on SHORT / single-sentence text — a few-token reword can flip many fingerprint bits, so Hamming<=3 either over-merges (loses distinct claims) or under-merges (misses rewords) at sentence granularity. The Hamming<=3 rule is tuned for ~document length, not sentences.
candidates.integration_point: Comparison row only; NOT recommended for sentence-level paraphrase collapse. POLARIS already computes it in ContentDeduplicator but uses MinHash for the actual similarity decision.
candidates.pros: Extremely compact (single 64-bit int), proven at Google web-crawl scale, sovereign.
candidates.cons: Weak on short text (the exact regime here). Hamming threshold not meaningful at sentence length. Rank LOW for this task despite being in-tree.

candidates.name: ROUGE-overlap thresholds (ROUGE-N / ROUGE-L)
candidates.primary_source_url: https://aclanthology.org/W04-1013/ (Lin, ROUGE: A Package for Automatic Evaluation of Summaries)
candidates.approach: Deterministic. ROUGE-N = n-gram recall overlap; ROUGE-L = longest-common-subsequence overlap. Threshold a pair as near-duplicate above a high ROUGE score.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: DROP: rewords with a shared contiguous clause score high ROUGE-N/L. KEEP (lexical): distinct claims score low. Same NUMBER/ENTITY blindness as all lexical methods -> carve-out required for the distinct-statistic KEEP. ROUGE is also documented PARAPHRASE-BLIND (penalizes synonym variation), so it is essentially asymmetric Jaccard with no advantage over symmetric n-gram Jaccard for dedup.
candidates.integration_point: Comparison row only. n-gram Jaccard or TF-IDF cosine dominate it for symmetric pair dedup; ROUGE is a summarization-eval metric repurposed, not a dedup primitive.
candidates.pros: Deterministic, sovereign, ROUGE-L's LCS tolerates word-insertion between shared tokens.
candidates.cons: Designed for summary-vs-reference eval, not symmetric dedup; recall-oriented (asymmetric); paraphrase-blind per its own literature; no edge over Jaccard/TF-IDF here. Marked unverified: ACL Anthology URL is canonical but not fetched this session. Lowest-ranked classic for this task.

recommended_shortlist: HYBRID LAYERED design (recommended): (1) same-citation-set gate FIRST -> (2) deterministic text-similarity collapse within the group via n-gram Jaccard >=~0.6 OR TF-IDF cosine >=~0.8 -> (3) number/ENTITY carve-out overrides any drop. Fully sovereign, deterministic, surgical extension of the existing dedup_same_span_sentences/_compose_section_per_basket — relax the grouping key from exact-offset footprint to same-EV_ID-SET.

OPTIONAL semantic upgrade for heavy synonym-paraphrase: Qwen3-Embedding (0.6B) cosine on the VM GPU, threshold cosine >=~0.93-0.97, layered on the SAME citation gate AND the number/entity carve-out (mandatory — embeddings are number-blind: the lethal distinct-statistic false-drop trap). Anchor frontier-date to Qwen3-Embedding 2025 + NeMo Curator 2025 (eps 0.01-0.1 == cosine 0.99-0.90).

Number/ENTITY carve-out is the method-INDEPENDENT faithfulness guarantee and must wrap whichever collapse method is chosen; extend the current number-only _number_tokens to also keep distinct named-entity/rare content tokens.
research.notes:
CORE FINDING (verified against primary sources + the actual POLARIS code at C:/POLARIS/src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py): NO similarity method separates 'same claim, different words, same citations' (DROP) from 'distinct claim / distinct number-entity' (KEEP) on its own. The separation is a TWO-PART guarantee independent of the similarity primitive: (A) the SAME-CITATION-SET gate (different sources = different basket = never collapse, §-1.3 basket faithfulness — this is half-built today in seen_numbers_by_footprint/idx8 but currently keyed on EXACT (ev_id,start,end) footprint, which is exactly why paraphrases at slightly different offsets EVADE it; relax the key to same-EV_ID-SET), and (B) the NUMBER/ENTITY carve-out (a sentence introducing a distinct number or entity token absent from kept siblings is KEPT even when flagged similar). Rank similarity methods only by how well they CATCH rewords; the carve-out is what makes any of them faithful.

SCALE: this runs on tens-to-low-hundreds of sentences WITHIN a section, post-composition. MinHash/LSH (sub-linear) and SimHash (64-bit Hamming<=3) are web-scale-crawl tools; at this scale exact n-gram Jaccard and TF-IDF cosine are trivial and have ZERO approximation error, so they DOMINATE the in-tree MinHash/SimHash despite those being already imported (do not over-rank by availability). SimHash is documented-weak on short/single-sentence text -> ranked low. ROUGE is paraphrase-blind by its own literature and offers no edge over symmetric Jaccard -> lowest classic.

LEXICAL vs SEMANTIC: the lexical methods (Jaccard/TF-IDF) catch the POLARIS sample (the paraphrases share an identical tail clause 'probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations'). They MISS pure-synonym rewords with no shared n-grams ('exposure measure chosen' vs 'exposure measure adopted'). Only sentence-embedding cosine (Qwen3-Embedding, open-weight, VM GPU, 2025-frontier-verified) catches those — but embeddings are NUMBER-BLIND (the lethal trap: '20%' vs '35%' score ~0.99 cosine and would be wrongly collapsed), so the carve-out is mandatory and load-bearing precisely for the embedding method.

THRESHOLD COMPARABILITY: NeMo Curator's eps is cosine DISTANCE -> eps 0.07 == cosine_sim ~0.93, eps 0.01 == ~0.99. POLARIS ContentDeduplicator uses 0.85 MinHash-estimated word-shingle Jaccard. Stated all thresholds in cosine-similarity / Jaccard terms for comparison.
