TF-IDF (sklearn) and ROUGE (Lin 2004 ACL W04-1013) URLs are canonical but were not fetched this session -> marked UNVERIFIED.

DELTA vs CURRENT CODE: dedup_same_span_sentences (verified_compose.py:84-140) carve-out is NUMBER-ONLY; the task asks number/ENTITY -> recommend a deterministic entity proxy (capitalized/rare content tokens absent from the kept sibling, conservative: any doubt -> KEEP). And the dedup key is exact-offset footprint -> relax to same-EV_ID-SET so offset-shifted paraphrases land in one group."

candidates.name: Bidirectional / mutual-entailment NLI redundancy collapse (IN-TREE: consolidation_nli.py, cross-encoder/nli-deberta-v3-base)
candidates.primary_source_url: https://github.com/MinishLab/semhash (for the semantic-over-lexical argument) + in-tree state/section_winner_board.md bake-off; method primary: https://huggingface.co/cross-encoder/nli-deberta-v3-base
candidates.approach: Two cluster representatives merge ONLY when entailment is the argmax of the 3-way cross-encoder logits in BOTH directions (A entails B AND B entails A) above a margin. Mutual entailment = semantic equivalence; one-directional entailment (subset/superset) does NOT merge. Deterministic union-find post-step over the gathered score matrix; bounded-parallel ThreadPoolExecutor. Already implemented and bake-off-validated in POLARIS (R=1.0/P=1.0 vs literal-floor R=0.0).
candidates.handles_our_shapes: PASSES the 2-groups-not-1 test, the discriminator here. FIX-D group-1 ('introduced/developed/employed a methodology to estimate P(computerisation) for 702 occupations') and group-2 ('the exposure measure was defined as P(computerisation)') do NOT mutually entail (the method-claim does not entail the definition-claim and vice-versa) so they stay SEPARATE = 2 survivors. WITHIN each group the 6 method-paraphrases mutually entail and the 5 definition-paraphrases mutually entail, so each collapses to one. Antonym flips score CONTRADICTION (argmax), free polarity safety; a number/entity-bearing sentence that is NOT mutually-entailed by a sibling is never merged. This is the only candidate whose failure mode (under-merge) is safe AND that also avoids the cosine over-merge trap.
candidates.integration_point: verified_compose (the producer of the paraphrase-padding) AND fact_dedup. Already wired as fact_dedup._build_nli_prose_groups via PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI + PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_PROSE, both DEFAULT-OFF. Recommendation: turn ON, confirm the verified_compose abstractive-writer sentence list actually reaches build_groups, and fire-test on the 11 FIX-D sentences (assert 2 groups) before production.
candidates.pros: Semantic, not lexical -- catches divergent paraphrases ('introduced/developed/employed') that Jaccard misses. Bidirectional requirement gives equivalence (not one-way entailment), which is exactly the right predicate for same-claim collapse and is the property that prevents cross-merging the two FIX-D groups. Open-weight deBERTa runs on the VM GPU (sovereignty OK). ALREADY BUILT and bake-off-proven in-tree -- near-zero new code, just enable + tune + fire-test.
candidates.cons: No clean EXTERNAL 2025-2026 primary publication anchors 'bidirectional-NLI-as-equivalence-dedup' specifically (the targeted-entailment 2025 paper arXiv:2508.17127 is one-directional; the FLAIRS mutual-implication paper is concept-support and was unverifiable/likely pre-2024). Evidence for POLARIS rests on the internal bake-off + the verified 2025 finding that token-level penalties cannot catch semantic paraphrase. Pairwise O(n^2) (bounded by PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_MAX_PAIRS=20000); a GPU model step adds latency vs pure-deterministic. Could spuriously partial-entail across the two FIX-D groups on the shared phrase -- hence the mandatory fire-test.

candidates.name: Content-word shingle near-dup (Jaccard 0.82 + polarity guard) (IN-TREE: fact_dedup._build_prose_groups)
candidates.year: 2026 (in-tree, I-beatboth-011 #1289; MinHash/Jaccard near-dup lineage is classic but the polarity-guarded variant is current)
candidates.primary_source_url: in-tree src/polaris_graph/generator/fact_dedup.py:_build_prose_groups; lexical near-dup baseline reference https://arxiv.org/abs/2107.06499 (Lee et al., dedup training data)
candidates.approach: Lowercase -> strip citation tokens -> drop stopwords -> word unigrams+bigrams shingle set -> exact Jaccard >= 0.82 clusters near-identical restatements. A deterministic polarity signature (negation cues, n't, UP/DOWN direction antonyms) must match exactly before two sentences may cluster, so 'raised' vs 'lowered' or an inserted 'not' blocks the merge even at Jaccard ~1.0. Clusters with >=2 occurrences (intra OR cross section) route through the keep-all cross-ref rewrite.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Under-merge (SAFE) on the 2-groups-not-1 test. Will NOT cross-merge the two FIX-D groups: only the 'probability computerisation' bigram is shared, so cross-group Jaccard is far below 0.82 -> 2 survivors guaranteed. WITHIN group-1 the 6 method-paraphrases may dip below 0.82 because the verb varies ('introduced/developed/employed/the investigation employed') -> SOME repetition can survive (incomplete collapse). Bigrams add order-sensitivity; the polarity guard prevents antonym false-merges. Safe but may leave residual repeats that NLI would catch.
candidates.integration_point: fact_dedup (build_groups), gated PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE, DEFAULT-OFF. Operates on empty-numeric-signature sentences -- the FIX-D sentences ('702' is not a year/%/$ so their FactSignature is empty) DO route here. Best used as the cheap pre-filter feeding the NLI leg.
candidates.pros: Fully deterministic, CPU, zero model, sub-millisecond at hundreds-of-sentences scale -- ideal sovereignty and run-speed. Polarity guard already addresses the lethal antonym-merge case. ALREADY BUILT in-tree. Cannot cross-merge the two FIX-D groups (its failure mode is the safe direction). Excellent cheap first-pass before the NLI model leg.
candidates.cons: Lexical, not semantic: divergent paraphrases that change content words ('introduced' vs 'employed', re-ordered clauses) can fall below 0.82 and survive -> incomplete collapse (this is precisely why the NLI leg exists). Threshold is a tuning knob; lowering it to catch more paraphrase raises cross-merge risk. Bigram Jaccard is sensitive to small edits.

candidates.name: Writer-side prompt constraint: 'state each distinct claim once' on the abstractive writer
candidates.approach: Fix the defect at the SOURCE instead of cleaning up after. The abstractive writer pads ('introduced/developed/employed a novel methodology' x6) because scope/format are under-specified -- documented padding-by-paraphrase behavior. Add an explicit instruction + few-shot to the writer's system prompt: enumerate each DISTINCT verified claim exactly once, do not restate the same claim with synonyms, prefer ONE sentence per (subject,predicate,object) basket. Optionally a soft length/claim budget per section.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: Prevents the FIX-D pattern from being GENERATED: a writer told 'one sentence per distinct claim' emits the methodology claim once and the exposure-measure claim once = 2 sentences, no padding. Does NOT risk cross-merge (it never collapses anything; it just doesn't over-produce). Faithfulness-safe by construction: it cannot drop a distinct claim because it is an instruction to the generator, not a post-hoc deletion. Best paired with a downstream dedup safety net (Jaccard+NLI) because prompt adherence is probabilistic, not guaranteed.
candidates.integration_point: abstractive_writer.py (the existing generator-role LLM invocation governed by polaris_runtime_lock.yaml -- NO new model). Complements, does not replace, the verified_compose/fact_dedup post-hoc dedup.
candidates.pros: Cheapest, fixes the root cause (over-generation) rather than the symptom. No new model, no new dependency, uses the existing generator call. Cannot violate faithfulness (no deletion). Reduces tokens/cost. Eliminates most padding before any dedup runs, so the downstream dedup load drops.
candidates.cons: Prompt adherence is probabilistic -- not a guarantee, so it MUST be backed by the deterministic+NLI dedup safety net (never the sole fix). Token-level decoding penalties (frequency_penalty/DRY/no_repeat_ngram) are NOT a substitute -- verified that they operate on token identity and cannot catch semantic paraphrase. Effect varies by model; needs a small A/B on the FIX-D section to confirm.

candidates.name: SemHash (MinishLab) -- embedding-cluster cosine semantic dedup
candidates.approach: model2vec static embeddings + Vicinity ANN -> cosine-similarity threshold (e.g. 0.9) clusters near-duplicates, keep one representative per cluster. CPU-fast (1.8M records in 83s; SQuAD-2.0 130k in 7s). MIT-licensed. Designed for training-set / dataset semantic dedup, also usable cross-dataset.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: OVER-MERGE RISK on the 2-groups-not-1 test -- the disqualifier for core use. The two FIX-D groups share 'probability of computerisation'; static-embedding cosine of group-1 vs group-2 sentences may exceed a single global threshold (0.9) and COLLAPSE them to 1 representative -> a distinct claim is dropped -> faithfulness-lethal in clinical context. Cosine has no equivalence/direction notion and no built-in polarity or cross-claim precision control (confirmed: docs do not expose cross-claim precision controls). Keeping it safe would require a per-pair threshold that, set high enough to avoid cross-merge, also stops catching the in-group paraphrases.
candidates.integration_point: Comparison row only. Could sit at fact_dedup as an alternative pre-filter, but its over-merge failure mode is the forbidden direction (claim loss), so it should NOT be the core dedup. Useful as a fast first-stage CANDIDATE generator feeding the bidirectional-NLI confirmer (cosine recall + NLI precision), never as the final merge decision.
candidates.pros: Genuinely 2025-2026 frontier, MIT, open-weight, CPU-fast, sovereignty-clean. Static-embedding speed makes it an excellent high-recall candidate generator. Mature API (self_find_representative, cross-dataset).
candidates.cons: Cosine threshold cross-merges distinct claims that share a phrase = the exact FIX-D failure = silent claim drop = faithfulness violation. No bidirectional/equivalence semantics, no polarity guard, no cross-claim precision control. Wrong as a STANDALONE merge decision for clinical-safety dedup; safe only as recall feeding an NLI confirmer.

candidates.name: SemDeDup (Abbas et al.) + corpus-dedup family (D4, SoftDedup, LSHBloom, NeMo Curator SemDedup)
candidates.year: SemDeDup 2023; SoftDedup ACL 2024; LSHBloom 2024 -- pre/at the 2024 boundary, baselines only
candidates.approach: k-means cluster embeddings -> within-cluster pairwise cosine -> drop above-threshold semantic duplicates, keep one representative (SemDeDup). SoftDedup REWEIGHTS (n-gram 'commonness' sampling weights) rather than removing. LSHBloom = MinHash-LSH with Bloom filters for internet-scale near-dup. All are TRAINING-CORPUS / DATASET-pruning altitude (millions-billions of documents), not sentence-level report composition.
candidates.handles_our_shapes: WRONG ALTITUDE/GRANULARITY for FIX-D. These operate on document/sample-level dedup for pretraining-data pruning, not on collapsing paraphrase-padding within one rendered report section. SemDeDup itself is the cosine-threshold method that, applied at sentence level, would over-merge the two FIX-D groups (same trap as SemHash, which is the modern productized descendant). SoftDedup reweights training samples -- not applicable to a finished report. Listed for completeness/comparison, not as integration candidates.
candidates.integration_point: None for the report-dedup problem. Conceptual ancestry only (SemDeDup -> SemHash). NeMo Curator is a GPU pipeline for corpus curation, not in-pipeline report dedup.
candidates.pros: Foundational, well-cited, open implementations exist (NeMo Curator GPU pipeline). Establish the embedding-cluster baseline the project's approaches improve on. SoftDedup's reweight-don't-delete philosophy aligns conceptually with POLARIS consolidate-keep-all (a comparison talking point).
candidates.cons: Pre-2024 / boundary baselines (SemDeDup 2023, SoftDedup/LSHBloom 2024) -- baselines only per the frontier mandate. Wrong granularity (corpus/training-data, not report sentences). SemDeDup's cosine-threshold core has the over-merge claim-loss failure mode. Not a viable core integration for FIX-D.

recommended_shortlist: Writer-side prompt constraint 'state each distinct claim once' on abstractive_writer.py -- fix the root cause (over-generation) cheaply; faithfulness-safe by construction (no deletion); must be backed by a downstream dedup safety net since prompt adherence is probabilistic

Content-word shingle Jaccard 0.82 + polarity guard (IN-TREE _build_prose_groups, enable PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE) as the deterministic cheap PRE-FILTER -- cannot cross-merge the two FIX-D groups (safe under-merge failure mode); catches the near-identical restatements

Bidirectional / mutual-entailment NLI (IN-TREE consolidation_nli.py, enable PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI + PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_PROSE) as the SEMANTIC confirmer -- passes the 2-groups-not-1 test (method-claim and definition-claim do not mutually entail -> kept separate), catches divergent paraphrases Jaccard misses, antonym = CONTRADICTION gives polarity safety, open-weight on VM GPU
research.notes:
PUNCHLINE: two of the three recommended fixes are ALREADY BUILT in-tree and DEFAULT-OFF -- this is mostly ENABLE + TUNE + CONFIRM-WIRING, not adopt-new-tech. (a) Jaccard prose-dedup = fact_dedup._build_prose_groups, gated PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE. (b) Bidirectional-NLI = synthesis/consolidation_nli.py wired via fact_dedup._build_nli_prose_groups, gated PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI + PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_PROSE. The only genuinely NEW build is the writer-side prompt constraint at abstractive_writer.py. Recommended stack = writer prompt-constraint (prevent over-generation) -> Jaccard pre-filter (cheap deterministic) -> bidirectional-NLI confirmer (semantic, equivalence not one-way). All route through the existing keep-all cross-ref rewrite, so corroborating citations are preserved (consolidate-keep-all, never drop a distinct claim).

RANKING RULE (the discriminator): the FIX-D sample is TWO distinct claims sharing the phrase 'probability of computerisation'; correct result = 2 survivors, not 1. OVER-MERGE (collapsing the two groups) = distinct-claim loss = faithfulness-LETHAL in clinical context = FORBIDDEN. UNDER-MERGE (leftover repetition) = SAFE/incomplete. Therefore: cosine-threshold methods (SemDeDup, SemHash) are comparison-rows-only -- their over-merge failure mode is the forbidden direction. Jaccard under-merges (safe). Bidirectional-NLI is the balance: within-group paraphrases mutually entail and collapse; the method-claim does NOT mutually entail the definition-claim so the two groups stay separate. Token-level decoding penalties (DRY/no_repeat_ngram/frequency_penalty) are DISQUALIFIED: verified-primary-source confirms they operate on token identity and cannot catch semantic paraphrase ('introduced/developed/employed').

HONESTY FLAGS (frontier mandate): I did NOT find a clean external 2025-2026 primary that specifically validates bidirectional-NLI-as-equivalence-dedup. The 2025 targeted-entailment paper arXiv:2508.17127 (Sulc) is ONE-DIRECTIONAL/targeted and by its own abstract 'does not address detecting when two sentences make equivalent claims' -- so it is NOT support for the bidirectional approach (included nowhere as such). The FLAIRS 'Mutual Implication as a Measure of Textual Equivalence' paper 403'd (UNVERIFIED) and is likely pre-2024 concept-support, not a frontier anchor. The bidirectional-NLI candidate therefore rests on the in-tree bake-off (state/section_winner_board.md R=1.0/P=1.0 vs literal-floor R=0.0) PLUS the verified 2025 sources establishing semantic-over-lexical necessity -- stated honestly rather than propped on a shaky cite.

DETERMINING WIRING CHECK (open, do not assume): FIX-D is INTRA-section. The numeric build_groups path requires >=2 DISTINCT sections so it misses intra-section repetition; but _build_prose_groups clusters on >=2 occurrences intra OR cross, so it CAN catch FIX-D -- IF the verified_compose._compose_section_per_basket abstractive-writer sentence list is actually the list fed to build_groups/dedup_pass. Confirm that data-flow before concluding 'just enable the flag'; if verified_compose output bypasses dedup_pass, a new integration at verified_compose is needed instead. ('702' confirmed empty FactSignature -- not %/$/19xx-20xx -- so FIX-D sentences route to the prose/NLI path, not the numeric path.)

CHEAPEST DE-RISK (project §-1.4 fire-test discipline): before trusting NLI in production, run consolidation_nli.group_clusters on the exact 11 FIX-D sentences and ASSERT it yields 2 groups (not 1, not 11). The bidirectional-argmax margin should prevent cross-group partial-entailment on the shared phrase, but this converts 'should work' into 'verified on our shape' for near-zero cost. Also confirm dedup_same_span_sentences (existing same-footprint collapse) does NOT already handle these -- it does not, because each paraphrase is anchored to slightly different (ev_id,start,end) offsets, which is the stated FIX-D evasion."
